description: Comprehensive SAT preparation course covering Reading & Writing (textual analysis, evidence, grammar, and rhetoric) and Math (algebra, advanced math, data analysis, geometry, and trigonometry). Designed to prepare students for all sections of the SAT.

topics.name: Unit 1: Central Ideas and Details
topics.description: Identifying main ideas, key details, and central themes in passages. Distinguishing between main ideas and supporting details, summarizing texts, and understanding author's primary purpose.

topics.name: Unit 2: Command of Evidence
topics.description: Evaluating evidence that supports claims, selecting textual evidence to support conclusions, interpreting quantitative evidence (charts, graphs), and assessing reasoning validity.

topics.name: Unit 3: Inferences and Text Structure
topics.description: Drawing logical inferences from passages, analyzing text organization and structure, understanding relationships between ideas, and recognizing authors' rhetorical moves.

topics.name: Unit 4: Words in Context
topics.description: Determining meaning of vocabulary words from context clues, analyzing word choice and connotation, understanding figurative language, and interpreting academic and domain-specific vocabulary.

topics.name: Unit 5: Rhetorical Synthesis and Transitions
topics.description: Integrating information from multiple sources, using effective transitions between ideas and paragraphs, rhetorical strategies for cohesion, and synthesizing notes into cohesive passages.

topics.name: Unit 6: Boundaries and Grammar
topics.description: Standard English conventions: subject-verb agreement, pronoun usage, punctuation (commas, semicolons, colons, dashes), sentence boundaries (fragments, run-ons), and modifier placement.

topics.name: Unit 7: Algebra
topics.description: Linear equations and inequalities, systems of linear equations, linear functions and their graphs, interpreting linear models, and solving algebraic word problems.

topics.name: Unit 8: Advanced Math
topics.description: Quadratic equations and functions, polynomial operations and factoring, rational expressions and equations, radical and exponential equations, function notation and transformations, and nonlinear systems.

topics.name: Unit 9: Problem Solving and Data Analysis
topics.description: Ratios, proportions, and unit conversions, percentages and percent change, data interpretation from tables and graphs, scatterplots and relationships, statistics (mean, median, standard deviation), and probability.

topics.name: Unit 10: Geometry and Trigonometry
topics.description: Area, volume, and surface area formulas, angle relationships and triangles, circles (arcs, sectors, equations), right triangle trigonometry, sine, cosine, and tangent ratios, and coordinate geometry.
